codeReview.summary: The feature adds the requested sync and checksum APIs plus solid baseline tests, but two correctness gaps remain: verifySync is hard-coded to GNU sha256sum even though the supported fleet includes a macOS host, and syncSkill rebuilds its own SSH transport for rsync instead of honoring the full HostConfig used by the SSH executor.

issues.description: verifySync hard-codes `sha256sum` for the remote checksum pass (`cd ${remoteSkillPath} && find . -type f -exec sha256sum ...`), and the local side uses the same GNU-only tool at `live.ts:278-303`. The mission/environment explicitly includes `shuvbot` as a macOS host (`mission.md:122`, `.factory/library/environment.md:13`), but stock macOS provides `shasum -a 256`, not `sha256sum`. As written, checksum verification fails on a supported host unless extra coreutils are installed, so VAL-SKILL-004 is not reliably implemented across the documented fleet. The added verify tests only mock `sha256sum` output (`packages/skill-ops/test/skill-ops.test.ts:845-1098`), so this portability bug is currently untested.

syncSkill reconstructs its own `ssh` command for rsync (`live.ts:202-204`) instead of reusing the SSH executor's transport settings. That rebuilt command carries timeout/port/user, but it drops `host.keyPath`, even though HostConfig supports that field (`packages/core/src/schema.ts:22,35`) and the shared SSH implementation explicitly passes it through with `-i` (`packages/ssh/src/live.ts:34-35`). On hosts that require a non-default key, the preparatory `ssh.executeCommand()` calls succeed while the actual rsync transfer uses the wrong credentials and fails, so the feature does not honor valid host configuration.

The new sync coverage never exercises a successful transfer or asserts that executable permissions survive the copy. The sync tests at `packages/skill-ops/test/skill-ops.test.ts:700-816` only cover missing-skill and forced-rsync-failure paths, while the verify tests mock checksum text rather than proving a real synced tree matches. That gap made the transport/configuration regressions above easy to miss and leaves the "preserves permissions and structure" requirement only indirectly covered.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill requires test-first TDD, but this worker implemented the skill-ops source changes before adding or running the package tests. The handoff still records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`, so the shared skill-feedback signal is inaccurate for this run.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md:28-30` says to create the package test file BEFORE implementation. In the transcript skeleton for session `2bf07cbe-fca7-40a0-86cc-63ff9f795c20`, the first edit is to `packages/skill-ops/src/errors.ts` (followed by edits to `src/types.ts` and `src/live.ts`) before the first `npm test -- --filter @shuvdex/skill-ops` excerpt and before the later test-file work; the handoff JSON nevertheless sets `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` to true.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repo has a de facto SSH transport implementation in `packages/ssh/src/live.ts`, but the shared docs do not tell feature authors to reuse or mirror that transport configuration when invoking `rsync`/`scp` directly. This sync feature reassembled an SSH command by hand and immediately drifted from the canonical behavior (dropping `keyPath`, changing host-key policy).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `packages/ssh/src/live.ts:17-36` centralizes SSH flags including `-i host.keyPath`, while `packages/skill-ops/src/live.ts:202-204` builds a separate `ssh` command string for rsync. `.factory/library/architecture.md` documents service/layer patterns but does not capture a convention for sharing transport option construction across shell-based integrations.

summary: Reviewed commit `5ce458b` and the worker transcript for `skill-ops-sync`. The review fails because checksum verification is not portable to the documented macOS host and rsync sync does not honor `HostConfig.keyPath`, so the feature is not reliable across supported host configurations. I also noted that the worker's recorded skill feedback says the backend-worker TDD procedure was followed even though the transcript shows implementation before tests.
